title: Join free talk in Wilderness to share new caracal research
bodyText:
WILDERNESS NEWS - Groundbreaking research on the elusive caracal will be the focus of an upcoming talk presented by Dr Zanri Strydom and hosted by Save the Wild in Wilderness next month.


> The team at Dunefields Private Nature Reserve on Paradise Ridge has become the first to develop a way to identify individual caracals, despite the species’ lack of distinctive spots, by using a completely non-invasive method.


This work is shedding new light on the caracals living in the region, revealing how many roam the area and what prey species sustain their populations in the local dune ecosystem.

Residents and visitors are invited to learn more at a special presentation taking place at the Fairy Knowe Hotel on Tuesday 23 September.17:30 – Arrival18:00 – Welcome18:15 – Presentation18:45 – Q&A sessionThe event is free of charge, but donations to the Kouga Kamma Conservation Foundation (KKCF NPC) are welcome.

Seating is limited, so attendees are asked to RSVP by 19 August to [email protected].

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
